description:
Please complete parts 1-3 of the PowerBI LinkedIn Learning course and then answer the following questions.

 

Note: You can access LinkedIn Learning free as BYU students, you don't need to sign up for a free trial, and you don't need to pay for it.

Log out of your "real" LinkedIn account that is attached to your personal email.

Then go to the LinkedIn Learning page and click to sign in. Simply enter your BYU Net ID student email address. That will prompt you to pick which BYU you are at (so don't pick Hawaii, Idaho, or Pathway). once you choose BYU, it will take you to the usual BYU SSO page, and you'll be in. I printed the screen below so you know what it will look like.

Once in, do parts 1-3 as described in the quiz instructions. If you have any trouble, just reach out to me or a TA.
name: Quiz 1 - Data Visualization with PowerBI Intro LinkedIn

description:
Complete the stats lessons 11 thru 14 in the MBA Excel and Stats Primer MyEducator course

Take Post-Test 3: Business Stats in Excel. You can reference notes and readings while taking this test.

No submission is required here, you will get full credit for achieving a passing score of 75%+ on the Post-Test by the due date

  • Note: you do NOT need to re-read nor submit anything for units 1-2 on Excel basics, charting, or optimization, only the Business Stats from unit 3
name: Assignment - MyEducator Stats Primer

description:
Watch the LinkedIn Learning videos below (total of 1hr 10 min at 1X speed) then complete the questions in this Canvas quiz.

This is an open-book (open-video) quiz but must be done on your own.

A bit of guidance:

  • You don't need to memorize how to use factorials to compute permutations and combinations, but you should understand why permutations and combinations are different.
  • Understanding the concepts behind percentiles, conditional vs. independent probability, discrete vs. continuous variables, etc. are important! These help your analytics intuition and enable you to do the basic spreadsheet math to solve business problems.
  • You won't need to memorize how to compute a standard deviation, but you need to understand the underlying concept of standard deviation and how it affects the way you interpret something like a normal curve or a Z score.

LinkedIn Learning - Probability Theory
name: Quiz 2 - Probability Theory Intro LinkedIn

Decision Theory and EMV.pdf

description:
Pre-class reading and quiz

1. Make sure you can login to Qualtrics:  byu.qualtrics.com -- User your BYU Net ID and password to access your account

2. Use The Qualtrics handbook of question design.pdf to answer the questions below (this is open book)
name: Quiz 4 - Questionnaire Design Handbook Reading

description:
[Note: do this assignment on your own, don't wait for in-class time to start. Instructions and due date are now updated.]

Situation: A commercial drone manufacturer (DronR) introduced a consumer drone that allows parents to track their children. The product has been in the market for the 3 years and the marketing group wants a dashboard to track product performance. Although sales have trended upward, profitability has remained flat and may be increasing in variability. The company needs to improve profitability.

Download the following dataset and import it into Power BI: dronr-data.xlsx

Step 1: Explore the data in Power BI.

Step 2: Create a very short deck reporting on the following:

  • Why isn't profitability increasing? Make a Power BI report and test various visualizations to tell the story of why profitability isn't increasing. Show 2-3 of your most useful visuals in 1-2 slides and describe why they are helpful to identify/solve the profitability problem.
  • Keeping your most useful visuals, add 3 KPI's to your Power BI report page. Describe your KPI's and explain why you think they are relevant for the company to track.
  • Take a screenshot of your Power BI report in Edit mode (showing all the viz and data options) and paste it into a slide.
  • Finally, using your Power BI report, create a KPI dashboard that includes:
    1. A line chart of Dollar Sales and Profit (by week)
    2. The three KPI's you created above
    3. Any other data visuals or statistics you think would be relevant for the marketing team
    Take a screenshot of your KPI dashboard and drop it into the last slide of your report.

Upload your completed deck as a PPTX or PDF.
name: Assignment - KPIs & Power BI Drone Analysis

name: Quiz 5 - Qualtrics Survey Programming Basecamp Course

description:
Download Data Science Salaries.xlsx

Do analysis in Excel to answer the questions below.

To make it easier to grade, please put each question's analysis in its own sheet.

Highlight your answer on the sheet. When done, save and submit your workbook here.

 

Q1
1 point
Filter to salary in USD, employee residence in US, and company location in US.
How many employees are in the filtered, clean data?

 

IMPORTANT: Use the resulting clean data from Q1 as the data you analyze for the remaining questions.

 

Q2
2 points
Use a lookup formula and insert an additional column next to Experience Level that has the value labels.
Use COUNTA and COUNTIF to create a summary table with the % of employees at each experience level.

Q3a
3 points
Create a 4x3 table computing the average salary by Experience Level and Company Size.

Q3b
2 points
Which size company (S, M, or L) has the highest Executive-level salary? The lowest?

Q4
5 points
Create a new column with all the "Engineer" related Job Titles, labeling non-engineer jobs as "All Other Jobs".
Create a table, sorted by count, of all the Engineer jobs.
What are the top 3 Engineer jobs?

Q5a
3 points
Go back to looking at ALL Job Titles (not just Engineers).
What salary does an employee need to be in the top 10% of pay?

Q5b
2 points
Which Job Title is most common among the top 10% paid employees?

Q6
2 points
Looking only at Entry-level employees, which size company (S, M, or L) allows the highest Remote work? The lowest?
Note: for this analysis, just treat Remote Ratio as numeric and compute the average of the column.
name: Assignment - Data Coding Techniques
Assignment - Employment Survey
lock_explanation: This assignment is locked until Feb 5 at 8am.

description:
Industry Analytics Case Presentation

For your industry analytics group projects, research the analytics practices of a company and develop a case study about a specific analytics project conducted by the company. Choose a contact and industry of your preference. Interview at least one key employee about an analytics-related project they conducted.

The case study presentation should include the following elements:

  1. Describe the challenge the company was facing (ask questions that allow you to apply the TOSCA framework to this part of your report)
  2. Present the analytics solution that was implemented, including details of analytics approaches used or data they found especially helpful
  3. Summarize the outcome(s) for the firm
  4. Demonstrate what concepts from class they did apply, or could have applied to potentially have done even better analysis work

Presentation length: 7-minute presentation, keep it interesting and stick to the relevant points. Then be ready for a few minutes of Q&A.

Presentations will be staggered across lecture days, but everyone must submit their presentation slides by the due date before the first groups present.

 

The following resources are helpful to reference as you work on this assignment.

TOSCA Framework

TOSCA Framework

Download TOSCA Framework

Example Case Study Presentations

Note: These are decent examples but assignment specs were slightly different

SkywestLinks to an external site.

AmazonLinks to an external site.
name: Group Project - Industry Analytics Case Presentation

description:
Many post-MBA roles will require pulling data in various ways, whether through a point-and-click UI or writing database queries. We are using a MyEducator SQL primer so everyone has baseline SQL experience.

  • Go through all chapters of the online course and run the practice queries to help build comprehension
  • There will be one or more quick assessment questions after each chapter sub-section
  • There will be a handful of questions in each chapter review quiz
  • There will be about a dozen questions from the post test (which uses the Lorenzo Shipping Company database) - you are welcome to work through the chapters and examples with others, but please do this final post test assessment alone

You may use other online resources throughout the course, but I highly recommend you build some queries by hand to get better comprehension and practice, especially in the early portion of the course if you are new to SQL.

For this assignment, your grade will be based on the % score from the online course.
name: Assignment - MyEducator SQL Primer
Midterm Exam Units 1-2

description:
Use regression to predict tip percent (you will need to compute this variable) using Tips Data Regression.xlsxLinks to an external site..

Run analysis in Qualtrics Stats iQ

Review the class discussion slides and run through the various steps of model exploration and diagnostics.

  • Complete steps are in lecture slides
  • Regression checklist summary is also provided in the data analysis intro doc

Submit a 2-slide PPT deck 

SLIDE 1

  1. Explore the data and run an initial model (but don't report on model results in this slide).
  2. Walk through data issues you explored. What aspects of data were fine? What issues did you address? 
  3. Which variables (if any) did you remove from your final model and why?

SLIDE 2

  1. What is your final model fit? What is your opinion about its accuracy/usefulness?
  2. You should show some clean chart or table of relevant model result, but also explain the most important learnings from the analysis.
  3. Summarize what predicts better or worse tips in plain English as if to a stakeholder who isn't analytically savvy. 
  4. Note: in this short deck, focus on presenting with clarity.
name: Assignment - Regression on Tips with Stats iQ

description:
Do each analysis below and put the results and answers to questions in a short PPT. 

Start with these steps:

  • Use the Advertising Data Regression.xlsx data file and import it into an LLM
  • Set a random seed of 42
  • Split data 80/20 for Train/Test

 

SLIDE 1

Train a Linear Regression with Y = Sales and X = TV, Radio, Newspaper.

  1. Explore the data and the initial model. Should we transform any variables?
  2. Which variables (if any) should we remove through backward selection?
  3. Re-run model with any transformations or revised variables selected, if desired.
  4. What is the R2 value for the initial regression vs. final model?
  5. Output coefficients in a small chart or table, and a chart of actual vs. predicted Sales.
  6. What Sales predictions do we get for the following?
  1. TV = 150, Radio = 25, Newspaper = 20
  2. TV = 400, Radio = 10, Newspaper = 0
  3. TV = 10, Radio = 400, Newspaper = 0

 

SLIDE 2

Now create a Random Forest Regression.

Note: No need to do much data coding or manipulation here.

  1. What is the R2 value for the initial regression vs. final model?
  2. Output coefficients in a small chart or table, and a chart of actual vs. predicted Sales.
  3. What Sales predictions do we get for the following, and how do they compare to the regression model? Comment on any major differences you see.
  1. TV = 150, Radio = 25, Newspaper = 20
  2. TV = 400, Radio = 10, Newspaper = 0
  3. TV = 10, Radio = 400, Newspaper = 0

 

SLIDE 3

Have the LLM recommend other predictive methods that could improve your results. Try a couple.

Describe how regression, RF, and one of the other methods compared (e.g., R2 values, most important predictors).

Which would you recommend using and why?

 

SLIDE 4

Use the LLM code to re-run your regression or random forest in Google Colab or Anaconda.

Put screenshots of your results and code into this slide (it doesn't need to be beautiful, just enough to confirm you succeeded at running code outside the LLM).
name: Assignment - Regression on Ad Data with LLMs
Quiz 7 - Random Forest for Lead Scoring
lock_explanation: This assignment is locked until Mar 12 at 8am.

description:
Analysis setup: Recent economic factors have made the job market more competitive for several roles at Swire Coca-Cola, including Merchandisers, Order Builders, and Drivers. From experience, managers know that employees are more likely to stay with the company for a long time if they stay past 90 days, and our data confirms this. Swire Coca-Cola seeks to provide new employees the proper support and training to ensure their first 90 days on the job are rewarding and successful.

Use the dataset Swire Employee Retention Data.xlsx provided to build a predictive model forecast the likelihood that an employee will stay at least 90 days.

Each team member should work on the analysis independently, then the group came come together to create a final approach.

Create a short PPT report answering the questions below:
Slide 1: Try a variety of models to find the most predictive model.
  1. Report each model's accuracy and AUC
  2. Which model would you select as the best one to use and why?

Slide 2: How well does each factor help in predicting employees staying with the organization during their first 90 days? Create visual(s) clearly showing your findings.

Slide 3: Which one variable do you recommend Swire focus on to increase the likelihood of employee retention for the first 90 days? Give some thought to causality for this question. What are things they can do that will increase the likelihood of retention? Create a compelling case using any combination of words, visuals, simulations, etc. based on your model output.
name: Group Project - Swire Coca-Cola Employee Retention

description:
Purpose: First, get practical experience with cluster analysis. Second, heavily automate the process using LLMs and AI tools to go from data to insights to reports very quickly.

Use cluster analysis to create segments for Crio Bru using this data:

Crio Segmentation Data.xlsx

Follow these steps:

  1. Upload the Crio file to your LLM and describe for the LLM what is contained (raw data + data dictionary)
  2. Confirm the uploaded data size and do some basic data viz to better understand the data 
  3. Create K-means clusters with 3-4 segments (have LLM recommend the optimal number; don't forget to set a random seed)
  4. Summarize the results of the segmentation in a table (segments in columns, variables in rows) 
  5. Have LLM provide its own description of each segment
  6. Give the segments useful and memorable names (iterate with your LLM)
  7. Generate images that represent the segments (using your LLM or taking your descriptions to a different LLM you like better for images)
  8. Suppose that Crio can only target 1 segment. Which segment should they pick and why?
  9. Use your LLM to outline a short marketing plan for that segment to increase Crio sales, including a creative brief for a social media campaign
  10. Use your LLM or another presentation-building AI tool (e.g., Gamma, Beautiful, Tome) to produce a segmentation report...you will need to summarize your analysis (segment names, descriptions, etc.) then do some minor slide clean-up, including a few key images/outputs from your analysis in the report...aim for 2-3 slides summarizing segments side-by-side, then 1-2 deep-dive slides on each segment (~10 slides max)  

This assignment is all about combining your own analysis intuition and iterating with LLMs. Apply what you have learned in class but lean heavily on automation for this assignment, do not spend a lot of time manually building the report or doing analysis outside of your LLM!
name: Assignment - Crio Segmentation with LLMs

description:
Analyze Customer Experience text data from Eco Flower in an LLM:

Eco Flower Customer Experience.xlsx

Create a short 1-page document of recommendations with some supporting data/visuals/quotes based on the following analysis:

  • Word clouds of positive (like) and negative (need to improve) comments.
  • Find themes from both like + improve comments. Quantify the prevalence of the themes. Save them as new variables.
  • Predict Likelihood to Recommend (the 0-10 scale) using these new theme variables (as dummy-coded predictors).
  • Combining the analyses above, what recommendations would you make to Eco Flower?
    1. Come up with your own recommendations first. Then iterate with your LLM to dive deeper.
    2. Use the LLM to get example supporting quotes for the report, e.g., a quote or two from very pleased and very displeased customers to illustrate your recommendations. Find strong quotes with emotion, you might need to iterate with the LLM to get good candidates.
name: Assignment - Text Analytics Eco Flower CX

description:
Use the following conjoint simulator/analysis and data for this assignment:

https://byu.pdx1.qualtrics.com/conjoint-simulator/shared/SV_9WGBhe1eC7kAmma

  • Note there are two pairs subgroups available to analyze, 1) Need Sponsorship vs. US Worker and 2) Female vs. Male
  • Reminder: when you leave the simulator, it will usually reset to defaults when you return, so keep track of what you configure

Employment Conjoint Setup and Results.xlsx

  • Note this outlines the conjoint setup, contains the raw utilities for each respondent, and includes a few survey variables than can be used to explore the results

 

Investigate the questions below as individuals for a short time, then come together as a group to provide answers in a PPT with clear supporting data/charts. Create one slide per question.

  1. Use Conjoint Analysis and Utility Scores: Are there notable differences in what Females vs. Males find valuable in a job offer?
  2. Use Conjoint Analysis, Total Population, and Willingness to Pay: Which 3 attributes seem most valuable to upgrade from the base level offering?
  3. Use Conjoint Simulator, Total Population, and Preference Share: Do not alter Job 1 or Job 2 but add Job 3 to the configuration. Design Job 3 in any way you want, subject to these constraints:
    • It must be Visa Sponsored
    • Base Salary + other Costs cannot exceed $130,000
    • Minimize the % of people who choose None of the job offers (i.e., maximize the sum of Job 1 + Job 2 + Job 3)
  4. Stay in the Conjoint Simulator and keep the new configuration with Job 3 you just created: Compare the preferences of Need Sponsorship, Females, and Males for Job 1 vs. Job 2 vs. Job 3. Are there notable differences? What principles would you recommend the HR team follow when they design job offers for a diverse set of candidates?
  5. Use Conjoint Simulator, Total Population, and Preference Share: Assume HR policy requires that we offer a single package to all candidates. Delete Job 2 and Job 3. What configuration maximizes the share of Job 1 while keeping its Base Salary + other Costs at $135,000 or less.

 

Note: here are the attribute levels we count as having added costs above the Base Salary:
name: Group Project - Employment Conjoint Analysis
